evidence.scientific_clarity: Two-panel ROC+PRC layout directly embodies the brief; AUROC/AP annotated per method in legends; diagonal and prevalence baselines present; OursModel visually distinguished by weight and color.
evidence.typography: pdf.fonttype=42 and svg.fonttype='none' set in rcParams; Arial/Helvetica preferred with DejaVu Sans as fallback only; axis labels 9pt, tick labels 8pt, titles 10pt bold — all sensible; no Type-3 fonts in PDF.
evidence.palette: Okabe-Ito subset used (grey, sky-blue, amber, deep-blue); no red/green directional conflict; all four hues are colorblind-distinguishable; line-weight and dash-style add redundant encoding.
evidence.layout_density: 10x4 figsize with tight_layout(pad=1.5) fits both panels well; axes span 0..1 with small margins (-0.02, 1.02); minor deduction because pad=1.5 leaves slightly generous inter-panel gap but no gaping whitespace or overlap.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure exactly (ROC left, PRC right, prevalence=0.40, four methods); legends placed at conventional corners (lower-right / lower-left); top/right spines removed; clean publication look.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF is version 1.4 with 2 TrueType/Type42 font indicators and zero Type-3 fonts; SVG also produced with fonttype='none'; both are genuine vector outputs.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (39 KB), figure.png (186 KB @ 300 dpi, 3000x1200), figure.svg (33 KB), caption.tex (636 B), gen_figure.py (4.9 KB) all present and non-trivial.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption names both panels, explains reference lines, reports prevalence numerically (0.40), states sample sizes (n+=120, n-=180), calls out OursModel as best, and notes single-split evaluation — fully informative.

overall_strengths: Comprehensive typography hygiene: pdf.fonttype=42, svg.fonttype='none', Okabe-Ito palette, redundant line-weight encoding, and a post-generation Type-3 font check baked into the script.
Caption is publication-ready with a concrete numerical finding (prevalence=0.40, n+/n- counts), matching the figure content exactly and satisfying Reviewer 2 expectations.
overall_weaknesses: tight_layout(pad=1.5) introduces slightly more inter-panel whitespace than needed for a two-panel figure; pad=1.0 or 0.8 would tighten the layout without risk of overlap.
No data.json is retained alongside the outputs, so the figure is not fully reproducible from the working directory alone (minor provenance issue, not a display flaw).
